summary: Iteration 31: Retest of FDI Dental Chart centering fix using wrapper View approach. FAIL - The wrapper View with flex:1, justifyContent:center, minHeight:100% is NOT expanding to fill the scroll area. Content is stuck at TOP with ~702px empty space below.

ui_bugs.issue: Dental chart content is NOT vertically centered. Content appears at TOP with ~702px empty space below. The wrapper View style (flex:1, justifyContent:center, minHeight:100%) is NOT being applied correctly by React Native Web.
ui_bugs.selector: Wrapper View inside ScrollView at line 332
ui_bugs.fix_required: The inline style object { flex: 1, justifyContent: 'center', minHeight: '100%' } is not rendering correctly. In the DOM, the wrapper has flex: '0 0 auto' instead of flex: 1, and justifyContent: normal instead of center.

action_items: FIX REQUIRED: The wrapper View style is not being applied. The DOM shows flex: '0 0 auto' and justifyContent: 'normal' instead of the expected flex: 1 and justifyContent: 'center'

POTENTIAL FIXES: 1) Use StyleSheet.create() instead of inline styles, 2) Try flexGrow: 1 instead of flex: 1, 3) Use Platform.select() for web-specific CSS, 4) Apply CSS directly via web-specific style injection
critical_code_review_comments: The 'as any' TypeScript cast on line 332 may be causing issues with style processing
React Native Web doesn't properly translate { flex: 1 } to CSS flexbox properties in this context
The minHeight: '100%' is also not being applied (shows minHeight: '0px' in DOM)
context_for_next_testing_agent: The wrapper View fix (flex:1, justifyContent:center, minHeight:100%) is NOT working. DOM analysis shows the styles are being ignored - rendered as flex: '0 0 auto', justifyContent: 'normal', minHeight: '0px'. Need a different approach - either StyleSheet-based styles, web-specific CSS, or a completely different centering mechanism.

UI_Fix_2_Dental_Chart_Centering.description: Dental chart in Add Implant modal is NOT vertically centered - appears at TOP with ~702px empty space below

dom_analysis.wrapperView: Styles NOT applied - flex: 0 0 auto, justifyContent: normal, minHeight: 0px
dom_analysis.scrollView: Correct - flex: 1 1 auto, overflow: hidden auto, height: 1015px

UI_Fix_2_Dental_Chart_Centering.root_cause: React Native Web is not properly translating the inline style object { flex: 1, justifyContent: 'center', minHeight: '100%' } to CSS. The 'as any' cast may be interfering with style processing.

code_location.currentCode: <View style={{ flex: 1, justifyContent: 'center', minHeight: '100%' } as any}>
code_location.expectedBehavior: View should expand to fill ScrollView and center its children vertically

flows_tested: Analyze modal content positioning with detailed DOM inspection
screenshots: Modal showing content stuck at top with ~702px empty space below

rca_of_the_issue.problem: Content not vertically centered in Add Implant Position modal
rca_of_the_issue.applied_fix: Added wrapper View with style={{ flex: 1, justifyContent: 'center', minHeight: '100%' } as any}
rca_of_the_issue.why_fix_didnt_work: React Native Web does not properly translate inline style objects to CSS in this context. The 'flex: 1' becomes 'flex: 0 0 auto', 'justifyContent: center' becomes 'justifyContent: normal', and 'minHeight: 100%' becomes 'minHeight: 0px'. The 'as any' TypeScript cast may be preventing proper style processing.
reproduction_steps: 3. Click on Phase2 Test (#REG-P2B) - Phase 2 Approved status
4. Scroll down and click 'Add Implant Position'
5. Observe: Content at top, ~702px empty space below
recommended_fixes: 1. Use StyleSheet.create() for the wrapper styles instead of inline object
2. Try flexGrow: 1 and flexShrink: 1 separately instead of flex: 1
3. Use Platform-specific styling with Platform.select() for web
4. Apply CSS class directly for web environment
5. Consider using alignSelf and alignItems on parent containers
